recent.title: Recent projects
recovery.title: Resume your project?
recovery.body: LayerAI automatically saved your last session. You can restore the model, your goal and all generated settings.
analyzing.label: Analyzing model…
intent.title: Describe your goal
intent.subtitle: Express what you expect from this print, in a sentence.
intent.placeholder: E.g.: I want a very strong part for outdoor use…
intent.advancedHint: Need a preset mode (Ultra Quality, Figurine…) or a custom profile? Open the advanced options (⚙ icon at the top).
intent.example.strength: I want a very strong part
risk.bed_detachment: Small contact area with the bed relative to the part's height.
risk.warping: Large first-layer footprint - risk of corner lifting on materials prone to warping.
risk.ringing: Tall, thin geometry - risk of visible vibration artifacts (ringing) at high speed.
risk.fragile_thin_wall: Thin walls detected (down to {value} mm) - risk of breakage.
risk.unsupported_overhang: {value}% of the surface is a significant overhang - supports are likely needed.
risk.instability: The center of mass is close to the edge of the footprint - risk of tipping during printing.
risk.title: Detected risks
risk.viewerHint: Orange surfaces in the 3D view are the detected overhangs.
risk.bed_detachment.title: Limited bed adhesion
risk.bed_detachment.consequence: The part may detach or move before the print is complete.
risk.bed_detachment.action: Add a brim, clean the bed and slow down the first layer.
risk.warping.title: Warping risk
risk.warping.consequence: Corners may lift and deform the base of the part.
risk.warping.action: Use a brim, avoid drafts and set the bed temperature for the selected filament.
risk.ringing.title: Possible vibrations
risk.ringing.consequence: Visible ripples may appear on vertical walls.
risk.ringing.action: Reduce speed and acceleration, then check frame rigidity and belt tension.
risk.fragile_thin_wall.title: Thin, fragile walls
risk.fragile_thin_wall.consequence: Some areas may print poorly or break during use.
risk.fragile_thin_wall.action: Increase perimeter count or thicken these areas in the source model.
risk.unsupported_overhang.title: Unsupported overhangs
risk.unsupported_overhang.consequence: Affected faces may sag and have an uneven finish.
risk.unsupported_overhang.action: Enable the recommended supports, prefer organic supports or reorient the part.
risk.instability.title: Unstable part
risk.instability.consequence: The part may wobble or tip as its height increases.
risk.instability.action: Reorient the part, add a wide brim or stabilizing supports.
supports.title: Supports

plate.fits: {count} copies arranged automatically on the plate.
plate.overflow: Only {maxFit} copies fit on one plate. Enable "Multiple plates" below to print all {count} pieces across several plates.
plate.multiPlateHint: All plates are shown together in the 3D view. Export and print them one by one using the active plate below.
learning.badgeHint: This setting was adjusted based on your feedback on similar previous prints.
learning.summary: {count} setting(s) below were refined based on your feedback on previous prints.

comparison.title: Comparison vs standard settings
comparison.subtitle: Indicative estimates, not a slicer prediction.
comparison.batchTotal: Estimate for the batch of {count} copies: ≈{time} · ≈{weight} g total.
cost.title: Cost estimate
cost.emptyHint: Enter your spool price in Settings → Costs to see an estimate here.
invoice.title: Generate an invoice
invoice.noCompanyHint: Fill in your company information in Settings → My company first to generate an invoice.
diagnosis.title: Photo diagnosis
diagnosis.subtitle: Upload a photo of your print for an AI defect diagnosis and suggested corrections.
diagnosis.hint: For a reliable analysis: frame the defect area, good lighting, neutral background, sharp photo.
diagnosis.privacyNotice: Privacy: when you click “Analyze”, this photo is sent only to your selected AI provider. It is not sent to Bob59 and LayerAI does not retain a copy.
diagnosis.correctionsApplied: {count} setting(s) applied to the current configuration.
diagnosis.noConfigHint: Generate a configuration first (review screen) to be able to apply these corrections.
layerView.noModelHint: Import a model first to enable layer view.
feedback.title: Feedback / Suggestion
feedback.hint: Describe your suggestion or the issue you ran into. This will open your mail app with the message ready to send.
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Explain your suggestion, or describe the issue (what you did, what happened)...
feedback.opened: Your mail app opened with the message ready to send.
feedback.copied: Copied! Paste it into your mail app or webmail.
advanced.title: Advanced options
resize.title: Resize the model
resize.doesNotFit: ⚠ The model doesn't fit on the {printer} bed ({axes} exceeded).
help.title: Help & About
help.step1Body: Drag and drop an STL, OBJ or 3MF file, or browse your files. Then choose your printer and filament.
help.step2Body: Describe what you want in natural language ("strong part", "as fast as possible"…). The local AI translates this into settings.
help.step3Body: If the model exceeds the print volume, a warning appears automatically. The ⛶ icon in the left sidebar lets you resize at any time.
help.step4Body: Every parameter is explained with a confidence score. Open the project directly in PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, or Creality Print, or export a .3mf, a PDF report, or an .ini profile.
help.features.naturalLanguage: Describe what you need in natural language and get automatically generated settings, explained and scored by confidence.
help.features.overhangs: Automatic overhang detection, with a visual support preview customizable zone by zone.
help.features.multiPlate: Print multiple copies, with automatic distribution across several plates if needed (opt-in).
help.features.photoDiagnosis: AI photo diagnosis: identifies the defect on a failed print and suggests one-click setting corrections.
help.features.multiExport: Export compatible with PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, and Creality Print, plus a detailed PDF report.
help.features.invoicing: Invoice generation compliant with French law, fully independent from the PDF report.
help.features.costEstimate: Print cost estimate (material + electricity) and time, in readable units.
help.features.learning: Learns from your print feedback to refine future suggestions.
help.export.threeMf: "Open in [slicer]" and "Export as .3MF" produce a complete project, settings already applied, ready to slice in PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, or Creality Print. LayerAI detects your slicer's executable automatically (or asks once if needed).
help.export.iniAndJson: To import just the settings into an already-open project: "PrusaSlicer profile (.ini)" imports via File → Import Config in PrusaSlicer; "Bambu Studio profile (.json)" and "Creality Print profile (.json)" import the same way in their respective apps (same JSON format, since both slicers share the same underlying engine).
help.export.pdf: The PDF report summarizes the analysis, the settings, and their reasoning. Unlike an invoice, it can include the estimated print time.
help.costBody: Enter your spool price (and optionally your printer's power and electricity price) in Settings → Costs. Material cost = estimated part weight (in kg) × price per kg. Electricity cost (if entered) = printer power (kW) × estimated print time (h) × price per kWh. The total shows up directly on the review screen.
help.invoiceBody: Enter your company details in Settings → My company (legal status, SIRET, VAT...), then use the "Invoice" button on the review screen. Every invoice gets a gapless sequential number (a French legal requirement), the required legal mentions (VAT or exemption, late-payment penalties, flat recovery indemnity) are added automatically, and print time never appears on an invoice.
help.aiBody: By default, LayerAI understands your description using a 100% local, free rule engine — no data is sent anywhere. You can enable a cloud AI provider (Settings → API keys, with your own key) for two optional uses: interpreting more complex descriptions, and photo diagnosis (which requires a cloud AI capable of analyzing an image). In both cases, only the necessary data (text or photo) is sent to the chosen provider.
help.faq.q1: Does LayerAI work without an internet connection?
help.faq.a1: Yes, for all core functions (analysis, settings, export). Only photo diagnosis and the optional cloud AI mode require a connection.
help.faq.q2: Do I have to pay for anything?
help.faq.a2: No, LayerAI is entirely free, with no paid tier or locked feature.
help.faq.q3: Can I reuse my own settings from one print to another?
help.faq.a3: Yes: in Advanced options → My profiles, you can save a configuration and reapply it later.
help.faq.q4: What happens if my model is too big for the bed?
help.faq.a4: A warning appears automatically, and the resize tool (⛶ icon in the side rail) lets you adjust the size.
help.faq.q5: How do I report a bug or suggest an idea?
help.faq.a5: Use the "✉ Feedback / Suggestion" button at the top of the window: a form prepares an email ready to send.
help.tips.tip1: Start with a simple model to get familiar with the Import → Describe → Review → Export flow.
help.tips.tip2: The preset AI modes (Advanced options) are a good starting point if you're not sure what to write.
help.tips.tip3: Rate your print results (at the bottom of the review screen): LayerAI learns from your feedback to refine future suggestions.
help.tips.tip4: Always check the "Advanced settings" tab before printing a critical part.
help.aboutBody1: LayerAI is a 3D print-preparation assistant. It analyzes your model and automatically generates print settings for PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, and Creality Print from a natural-language description. By default, everything runs locally with a 100% offline, free rule engine. You can also opt in to an external AI provider (with your own API key, in Settings → API keys) to interpret more complex descriptions or for photo diagnosis — in that case, the relevant data is sent to that provider, never otherwise.
help.aboutBody2: The printer/filament profile databases are based on public PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, and CrealityPrint data (AGPLv3). See the docs/licensing folder for attribution details.
help.aboutThanksBody: Thanks to Keup's 3D and lolo.lc3d for the advice that helped improve this software.
onboarding.step0Body: LayerAI analyzes your 3D model and automatically generates print settings for PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, or Creality Print. Just describe what you want.
onboarding.step1Body: Drag and drop an STL, OBJ or 3MF file, or browse your files. Then choose your printer and filament.
onboarding.step2Body: "I want a very strong part", "as fast as possible", "it's a figurine"… The AI understands natural language and combines several intents at once.
onboarding.step3Body: The ⚙ icon at the top right opens the preset AI modes (Ultra Quality, Ultra Strong…), your custom profiles and all the raw settings, editable if needed.
onboarding.step4Body: Every generated parameter is explained with a confidence score. Then export a .3mf project ready to open in your slicer, a PDF report, or an .ini profile.
settings.title: Settings
settings.updates.checkOnStartup: Check for updates on app startup
settings.costs.intro: Enter your spool price to show a material cost estimate (and electricity, optionally) on the review screen.
settings.company.intro: This information appears on your invoices. Check it matches your actual legal situation before sending an invoice to a client.
settings.company.name: Name / company name
settings.language.title: Language
settings.theme.title: Theme
settings.apiKeys.cloudModeToggle: Use a cloud AI provider to interpret intent
settings.apiKeys.cloudModeHint: By default, LayerAI uses its 100% local rule engine (free, offline). Enable this to let one of the providers above interpret your text instead — useful for more complex phrasing. On failure, LayerAI automatically falls back to the local engine.
settings.apiKeys.noProviders: No provider configured. Add one above to enable optional cloud AI.
settings.apiKeys.privacyNotice: Your API keys are encrypted with Windows secure protection and are never displayed or sent to Bob59. Text or photos leave your computer only when you deliberately enable a cloud AI feature; they are then sent directly to the selected provider.
menuAction.noModel: Import a model first to run automatic optimization.
menuAction.noModelResize: Import a model first to access resizing.
menuAction.noModelHealth: Import a model first to check its integrity.
menuAction.meshValid: ✓ Valid mesh, no repair needed (analysis confidence: {confidence}%).
menuAction.meshInvalid: ⚠ Non-manifold mesh detected (analysis confidence: {confidence}%). Use the repair tool built into PrusaSlicer, Bambu Studio, or Creality Print before printing.
menuAction.autoOptimizeIntent: Find the best trade-off between quality, strength and print speed.
menuAction.updatesUnavailableDevDetail: This feature is only available in the installed version of LayerAI.
update.title: Updates
update.upToDate: You're already using the latest version.
update.devUnavailable: Update checking is only available in the installed version of LayerAI.
native.worker.exited: The analysis process stopped with code {code}.
native.provider.tooLarge: The provider response is abnormally large.
native.provider.noModel: No model is loaded in LM Studio. Load a model in LM Studio, then try again.
native.provider.emptyResponse: The provider returned an empty response.
native.secureStorage.saveUnavailable: Secure system storage is unavailable: the API key was not saved.
native.server.tooLong: The server address is too long.
native.server.credentials: The server address must not contain credentials.
native.server.localOnly: LM Studio must use a local server (localhost).
native.server.httpsRequired: A cloud provider must use an HTTPS address.
native.photo.invalidType: Unsupported image format. Use JPEG, PNG or WebP.
native.photo.tooLarge: The image must be smaller than 12 MB.
native.photo.signatureMismatch: The image content does not match its declared format.
native.export.invalidPdf: The generated PDF report is empty or invalid.
native.export.invalidPng: The generated PNG capture is empty or invalid.
native.import.notFile: The selected path is not a file.
native.import.tooLarge: The model must be smaller than 250 MB.

welcome.title: Thank you for using LayerAI!
welcome.description: LayerAI guides you from model analysis to settings and exports ready for 3D printing.
welcome.thanks: Thank you for trusting LayerAI and helping improve the project simply by using it.
welcome.support: If the software is useful to you, you can support its development through PayPal.
welcome.neverShowAgain: Do not show this window at startup again

settings.support.showWelcomeHint: The window always remains available from Help → Support LayerAI.
